page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-16-lssd is a virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs derived from 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The processor is equipped with a 504 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. System memory totals 60.0 GB, providing 3.75 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 539 GB of NVMe SSD capacity distributed across two local SSDs. No GPUs are included in this configuration, and it does not come with complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with the stress-ng single-core CPU test placing in the top 25% at 2301.48. Single-threaded tasks show high efficiency, as evidenced by top-tier results in Geekbench single-core (2297.0), single-threaded gzip compression (36.82 GB/sec), and single-threaded bzip3 decompression (15.64 GB/sec). Multi-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance (18700.11) and Geekbench multi-core score (13648.0) falling within the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth tests also show average performance, yielding 132.61 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 56.89 GB/sec for large-block write operations.

From a resource density perspective, the instance offers a balanced ratio of compute to memory alongside fast local NVMe storage. The inclusion of dedicated Intel Xeon cores with Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) supports accelerated vector and matrix operations, which is reflected in the top-tier text generation speed of 371.82 tokens/sec for small LLM models. However, the 3.75 GB of RAM per core may limit memory-intensive workloads. This hardware profile is suited for database operations, software compilation, web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference where local, low-latency storage is required.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-16-lssd is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs from 8 physical Intel Xeon 6985P-C cores, 60.0 GB of RAM, and 539 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Running at 2.3 GHz with a 504 MB L3 cache, the processor supports AVX-512 and AMX instructions. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core performance, including a Geekbench single-core score of 2297.0 and high single-threaded compression speeds, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average range. The instance provides a balanced compute-to-memory ratio with low-latency local storage, making it suitable for database operations, software compilation, and web serving workloads that do not require GPU acceleration.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-16-lssd features 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 60 GB RAM, and 539 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong single-core performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-16-lssd is an x86_64 instance with 16 vCPUs, 60 GB RAM, and 539 GB local NVMe SSD, optimized for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced virtual machine combining dedicated Intel Xeon processors and local NVMe storage for efficient single-threaded and database workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors
60.0 GB of system memory and 539 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and compression benchmarks
Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 instruction support
Suitable for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
